catalog.hint: Jamila master data — CSI spec sections, approved vendors, and electrical load schedules from the construction package.

site.intro: Site Engineer workspace — build only from approved drawings, log pours, track BOQ, and raise snags.
site.approvedHint: Only Approved for Construction drawings are shown. Superseded or pending issues are hidden.
site.boqHint: Log installed quantities against the contract BOQ. Exceeding planned qty requires a Variation Order.
site.defectCreated: Defect logged. High-severity defects can lock pour clearance.
site.rfisHint: Technical questions affecting site works. Cost-impact RFIs may require a Variation Order.

drawings.uploadHint: Resilient multipart upload with automatic retry on network drops. DWG preferred; DXF and PDF also accepted.
drawings.pendingHint: Review each drawing, then Approve for construction or Request Revision. PDF opens in a new tab; DWG/DXF downloads for AutoCAD.
drawings.enterNumberTitle: Enter drawing number and title before uploading.
drawings.chooseFileFirst: Choose a DWG, DXF, or PDF file first.
drawings.uploadInterrupted: Upload interrupted — retrying failed parts automatically.
drawings.uploadFailedFinal: Upload failed after automatic retries. Check your connection and try again.
drawings.downloadInterrupted: Download interrupted — resuming from last position…
drawings.requiredFields: Drawing number, title, and file are required.
drawings.directUploadFailed: Direct upload to object storage failed
drawings.dragDrop: Drag & drop a DWG file here, or
drawings.noDrawingsMatch: No drawings match the current filters.
drawings.confirmWithdraw: Are you sure you want to withdraw {number} · {title}? This cannot be undone.

models.dragDrop: Drag & drop CAD/PDF file here, or

structural.lockedNcr: ZONE LOCKED: Open Non-Conformance Report detected. Resolve before pour clearance.
structural.lockedMep: ZONE LOCKED: MEP Submittals (Sleeves/Embeds) are still pending. Cannot clear pour.
structural.checklistHint: All four checklist items must be approved before pour clearance.

execution.executionHint: 5D BIM field execution — actual quantities cannot exceed baseline BOQ without a Variation Order.
execution.costImpact: Cost impact — VO may be required

mep.hint: Predictive deviation analysis surfaces schedule and cost impact before owner override.
mep.ownerOverride: Requires Owner Override for Unlisted Vendor.
mep.createHint: Register MEP equipment, sleeves, or embeds for head engineer review. Include the zone code in the equipment tag to link pour clearance locks.
mep.equipmentTagHint: Include zone code (e.g. G1) so pending submittals lock the matching pour zone.
mep.missingRequired: Equipment tag and CSI division are required.
mep.selectVendorRequired: Select an approved vendor from the catalog.
mep.equivalenceRequired: Upload an equivalence letter for unlisted vendors.

admin.title: System Administration
admin.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this record? This cannot be undone.
admin.confirmDeleteDrawing: Are you sure you want to delete {number} · {title}? This cannot be undone.
admin.confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} selected drawing(s)? This cannot be undone.
admin.superAdminHint: Full system control — manage users, master data, BOQ, drawings, audit, and backups across all roles.
admin.catalogManageHint: Create and maintain CSI spec sections, approved vendors, and electrical load schedules.
admin.boqManageHint: Add, edit, or remove BOQ lines grouped by CSI division.
admin.drawingsManageHint: Manage the full drawing register — edit LOD metadata, approve pending submissions, or archive rows.
admin.fileLinkHint: Secure link via the API — opens or downloads the stored CAD/PDF without exposing MinIO directly.
admin.storagePathHint: Internal object key returned after upload — required when creating a new row.

chat.emptyThread: Say hello to start the conversation.
chat.globalChatHint: Everyone on the platform can see messages here.
chat.chatBanned: You are muted for 1 minute after 3 language violations.
chat.mutedUntil: Muted for {seconds}s due to language violations.

help.generalTip: Hover the help icon on each tab for a quick summary. The info banner below the tabs explains the active feature in more detail.

roleWelcome.SUPER_ADMIN: You manage the entire platform: users, master data, BOQ, drawing register, audit trail, and system backups. Use Pending Drawings to approve or reject consultant submissions — bulk delete is available here only.
roleWelcome.ADMIN: You support day-to-day administration: team access, catalog data, and drawing review workflows. Changes you make are recorded in the audit trail.
roleWelcome.PROJECT_MANAGER: You oversee project health: design reviews, site progress, and coordination between consultants and site teams. Drawings must be approved before site use.
roleWelcome.HEAD_ENGINEER: You are the technical gatekeeper on site. Review pending drawings and MEP submittals, run structural QA/QC before pours, track BOQ execution (5D BIM), and resolve RFIs and snags.
roleWelcome.SITE_ENGINEER: You execute work on site. View approved drawings only, log pours after head-engineer clearance, record BOQ progress, report defects, and raise RFIs when clarification is needed.
roleWelcome.CONSULTANT: Upload new drawing revisions (DWG, DXF, or PDF). Each new version automatically supersedes the previous one so site teams never build from outdated models.
roleWelcome.ARCH_CONSULTANT: Upload architectural drawings and sheets. Provide LOD metadata (sheet size, scale, package) so the register stays complete for reviewers and site teams.
roleWelcome.STRUCT_CONSULTANT: Upload structural drawings. Head engineers use your approved sheets for pour clearance and QA/QC checklists before concrete is placed.
roleWelcome.MEP_CONSULTANT: Submit MEP shop drawings and material submittals for head-engineer approval. Approved items become APPROVED FOR CONSTRUCTION; rejected items return as REVISION REQUESTED.

drawings.tip: Review consultant uploads before they reach site.
drawings.guide: Pending Drawings lists submissions awaiting your decision. Approve to release to site engineers, or reject with comments. New consultant uploads automatically supersede older versions of the same drawing number.

mep.tip: Approve or reject MEP shop drawings and material submittals.
mep.guide: MEP Submittals tracks mechanical, electrical, and plumbing packages. Review each submittal against the project spec section. Approve for construction or request revision. Owner deviations may require an additional approval step.

structural.tip: Structural QA/QC — pre-pour checklist and clearance.
structural.guide: Run the pour clearance checklist: formwork, rebar, post-tension (PT), and hold points. The system blocks approval if open NCRs, MEP conflicts, or site defects exist on the pour zone. Only clear pours that pass every gate.

execution.tip: Field Execution (5D BIM) — BOQ planned vs installed quantities.
execution.guide: Track Bill of Quantities (BOQ) progress: planned quantity, logged installs, and percent complete. Rows turn red at 100%. To exceed planned quantity you need a Variation Order (VO) — do not over-install without approval.

rfis.tip: RFIs and site snags — questions and defects to close.
rfis.guide: Manage Requests For Information (RFIs) from site and consultant snags. Answer RFIs to unblock work; track snags until closed. Open items may block structural clearance or pour approval.

catalog.tip: Project master data — CSI specs, vendors, load schedules.
catalog.guide: Maintain the project catalog: CSI spec sections, approved vendors, and electrical load schedules. MEP submittals and BOQ lines reference this data — keep it accurate before approvals.

boqAdmin.tip: BOQ Management — define quantity lines by CSI division.
boqAdmin.guide: Add and edit BOQ lines with unit, rate, and planned quantity. Site engineers log execution against these lines in Field Execution. Changes here affect progress reporting and VO thresholds.

team.tip: Invite and manage project team members.
team.guide: Create user accounts and assign roles (site engineer, consultant, etc.). Deactivated users lose access but records are preserved. Super Admin manages platform-wide access from the admin dashboard.

drawings.tip: Approved drawings only — safe to build from.
drawings.guide: This register shows drawings approved by the head engineer. Do not use superseded or pending sheets. Download the latest revision before starting work on site.

pours.tip: Log concrete pours after head-engineer clearance.
pours.guide: Record pour date, location, and volume once structural QA/QC clearance is granted. Pours logged without clearance may be flagged for audit.

boq.tip: BOQ Progress — log installed quantities in the field.
boq.guide: Update actual installed quantities against the BOQ. Progress feeds the 5D BIM view for the head engineer. Stop at 100% of planned quantity unless a Variation Order (VO) is approved.

defects.guide: Create site defect records with location, photo, and severity. Open defects can block pour clearance in the same zone. Close defects only after remedial work is verified.

rfis.tip: Raise RFIs when drawings or specs are unclear.
rfis.guide: Submit a Request For Information (RFI) with a clear question and reference drawing. Track status until the head engineer or consultant answers. Do not guess — RFIs protect quality and liability.

team.guide: Manage all users across the ERP. Assign roles, reset access, and deactivate accounts (soft delete). Every mutation is audit-logged.

catalog.tip: Master catalog — CSI, vendors, electrical loads.
catalog.guide: Edit project master data used by MEP, BOQ, and approvals. Keep CSI divisions and vendor lists aligned with the construction package.

boq.guide: Define and maintain BOQ items, units, rates, and planned quantities. Site execution and head-engineer dashboards read from this master list.

drawings.tip: Full drawing register with bulk actions.
drawings.guide: Review pending consultant uploads, edit LOD metadata, and archive records. Bulk delete is restricted to Super Admin. Approving releases drawings to site engineers.

audit.tip: Immutable audit trail of all mutations.
audit.guide: Every POST, PUT, and DELETE logs the user ID and action. Use this trail for compliance, dispute resolution, and security reviews.

backups.guide: Trigger on-demand backups before major migrations or data changes. Restore procedures should be tested on a staging environment first.

drawings.tip: Upload a new drawing revision to the vault.
drawings.guide: Submit DWG, DXF, or PDF files with drawing number, title, and discipline. Fill LOD fields (sheet size, scale, package) when available. Uploading a new version marks the previous revision as SUPERSEDED automatically.

mep.tip: Create MEP material and shop drawing submittals.
mep.guide: Link each submittal to a CSI spec section, attach files, and submit for head-engineer review. Status becomes APPROVED FOR CONSTRUCTION or REVISION REQUESTED. Resubmit after addressing comments.
